item.title: 17 of the Best Small Trees for Urban Gardens
item.description:
Tiny lots can be challenging for city dwellers with big landscaping plans, but luckily there are lots of small trees to choose from. Not all species thrive in cities though, so you’ll want to make your choice from a curated selection. Learn about 17 of the best small trees for urban yards and gardens in this guide.

The post 17 of the Best Small Trees for Urban Gardens appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Signs your avocado tree is healthy . . . or not
item.description:
May is usually an easy time of year for an avocado tree in Southern California. Therefore, signs of ill health now should be taken seriously and, if possible, remedied before the potential stress of heat or drought during summer arrives. In my yard this May I have some healthy and some not -so-healthy avocado trees. […]

The post Signs your avocado tree is healthy . . . or not appeared first on Greg Alder's Yard Posts: Southern California food gardening.
item.source:markdown:
May is usually an easy time of year for an avocado tree in Southern California. Therefore, signs of ill health now should be taken seriously and, if possible, remedied before the potential stress of heat or drought during summer arrives. In my yard this May I have some healthy and some not -so-healthy avocado trees. \[…\]
The post [Signs your avocado tree is healthy . . . or not](https://gregalder.com/yardposts/signs-your-avocado-tree-is-healthy-or-not/) appeared first on [Greg Alder's Yard Posts: Southern California food gardening](https://gregalder.com/yardposts).

item.title: Blueberry Recipes to Make During California Blueberry Season
item.description:
California blueberry season is one of spring’s sweetest signals. In California, blueberry harvest typically runs April through June, and because blueberries are allowed to ripen on the plant, growers harvest them multiple times throughout the season. That means spring is the perfect time to look for fresh California blueberries at the grocery store, farmers market […]

The post Blueberry Recipes to Make During California Blueberry Season appeared first on California Grown.
item.source:markdown:
California blueberry season is one of spring’s sweetest signals. In California, blueberry harvest typically runs April through June, and because blueberries are allowed to ripen on the plant, growers harvest them multiple times throughout the season. That means spring is the perfect time to look for fresh California blueberries at the grocery store, farmers market \[…\]
The post [Blueberry Recipes to Make During California Blueberry Season](https://californiagrown.org/blog/blueberry-recipes-to-make-during-california-blueberry-season/) appeared first on [California Grown](https://californiagrown.org).

item.title: How to Plant and Grow Red Currants
item.description:
Red currants are flavorful and full of nutrients. There are lots of species and cultivars to choose from of this underappreciated beauty, whether you need some bright autumn color, love vibrant spring blossoms, or want to enjoy the berries in tasty desserts and savory meals. Learn how to grow red currants in this guide.

The post How to Plant and Grow Red Currants appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Avocado talk and tasting at San Diego CRFG, Wednesday, May 27

item.title: Are Leafy Greens with Insect Holes Safe to Eat?
item.description:
Are leafy greens with bug holes okay to eat? They might be, but this is not the case if mammals have been nibbling on your crop. Shockingly, eating a raw slug or snail can also impact your health in rare cases. Read more now to learn when it is okay to eat greens from the garden that have been damaged by insects.

The post Are Leafy Greens with Insect Holes Safe to Eat? appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.description:

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle
item.description:
While Japanese honeysuckle is a favorite for pollinators, and known for being tolerant of neglect, it also has a reputation for unbridled growth and spreading out of control. In some areas, planting is discouraged or outlawed, but if you’re in a “safe zone,” this vine can bring the fragrance of summer to your garden.

The post How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
While Japanese honeysuckle is a favorite for pollinators, and known for being tolerant of neglect, it also has a reputation for unbridled growth and spreading out of control. In some areas, planting is discouraged or outlawed, but if you’re in a “safe zone,” this vine can bring the fragrance of summer to your garden.
The post [How to Grow and Care for Japanese Honeysuckle](https://gardenerspath.com/plants/vines/grow-japanese-honeysuckle/) appeared first on [Gardener's Path](https://gardenerspath.com).

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Hardy Cyclamen in the Garden
item.description:
Hardy cyclamen are herbaceous perennials that provide weeks of fragrant, red-toned to white flowers in autumn, winter, or early spring. Superb for shade gardens, the slow-spreading tubers form charming drifts that thrive under deciduous trees and shrubs. Learn how to grow and care for hardy cyclamen in this guide.

The post How to Grow and Care for Hardy Cyclamen in the Garden appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Ostrich Ferns
item.description:
Ostrich ferns thrive in shady spots, bringing a lush, woodland feel to gloomy corners of the garden. Their dramatic, vase-shaped fronds reach impressive heights, creating a bold green backdrop that feels wild. Low-maintenance once established, they return reliably each year. Learn how to grow ostrich ferns in this guide.

The post Learn How to Grow Ostrich Ferns appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Plant and Grow Black Currants
item.description:
Black currants are one of the garden's most rewarding fruiting shrubs. Rich in vitamin C and antioxidants, the berries are outstanding for jams, juices, and preserves. With cultivars suited to a range of climates and conditions, there's a variety for nearly every garden. Learn how to grow black currents in this guide.

The post How to Plant and Grow Black Currants appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Grow Flowering Quince for Early Spring Color
item.description:
Flowering quince is an easy-care shrub that produces vibrant flowers in late winter to early spring. Shades of pink, red, orange, and white adorn the thorny branches, signaling the end of winter, and some varieties even produce edible fruit. Learn how to plant and grow flowering quince in your garden. Read more now.

The post How to Grow Flowering Quince for Early Spring Color appeared first on Gardener's Path.
